message:
fix(runtime): enforce structural quiescence before region close and track finalizing regions precisely

region.rs — Two changes to enforce structured concurrency invariants:
  1. add_child() now uses can_spawn() instead of can_accept_work()
     for admission control, correctly reflecting that spawning children
     requires the region to be in a state where new sub-regions are
     allowed (not just accepting tasks).
  2. complete_close() now calls is_quiescent() before transitioning
     to Closed. Previously, the caller (advance_region_state) checked
     readiness, but the region itself did not guard the transition—so
     a concurrent path could close a region that still held live tasks
     or children, clearing its heap memory prematurely and violating
     structured concurrency guarantees.

state.rs — Three related changes:
  1. Replace `finalizing_region_count: usize` with
     `finalizing_regions: Vec<RegionId>`. The count was O(1) to update
     but required a full arena scan in drain_ready_async_finalizers()
     when count > 0. The Vec provides O(count) iteration without
     scanning the (potentially large) arena.
  2. can_region_complete_close() now requires task_count() == 0 and
     child_count() == 0 instead of checking if tasks are terminal.
     A terminal task whose task_completed() cleanup hasn't run yet
     still has outstanding obligations (heap references, obligation
     tracking). Closing the region before cleanup would clear the heap
     while those references are still live.
  3. Add child_count() > 0 check: child regions must be fully closed
     and removed before the parent can close, enforcing the
     parent-outlives-children invariant.

app.rs — Update three tests to satisfy strict quiescence: remove
tasks and children from regions before calling close lifecycle
methods, and assert on complete_close() return values.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
